relations: Myths have shaped societies by explaining the unknown, providing moral guidance, reinforcing social norms, and offering comfort
The tale of King Midas is a myth that serves as a moral lesson against greed
In ancient times, myths were used to explain natural phenomena and the unknown
Myths were often created to make sense of natural phenomena, such as the changing seasons and the movement of celestial bodies
The myth of Demeter and Persephone is part of Greek mythology, explaining the seasons
Hades' abduction of Persephone is a central event in the myth explaining the cycle of seasons
